title: Java Argument Passing: Pass-by-Value Mechanism
description: A flowchart illustrating how primitive and object reference arguments are passed by value in Java, showing the distinction between modifying a primitive copy and modifying the state of an object through its copied reference.
elements: Heap Object (the actual object instance in memory)
relationships: Calling Method passes a copy of the value of a primitive variable to the Method Parameter
Calling Method passes a copy of the value of an object reference (the memory address) to the Method Parameter
Both the original reference and the copied parameter reference point to the same Heap Object
Modifying the Method Parameter's primitive value does not affect the original variable
Modifying the state of the Heap Object through the parameter reference affects the original object
